title: Reader API Caching, ETags, Preconditions, and Invalidation · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Write Preconditions
summary: Personal timelines are cached publicly, validators omit tenant and filters, 304 responses ignore authorization changes, writes leave stale pages, and weak and strong tags are confused. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go write preconditions increment in privacy-safe conditional reader API. Learners progress through prediction, exp…
objectives: Apply If-Match or version preconditions to conflict-sensitive writes and return current recovery evidence on mismatch.
Evaluate If-None-Match precedence and If-Modified-Since correctly after authentication and representation selection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Write Preconditions · Theory Feed Go Write Preconditions
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 3755 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile private cache classification, ETags, conditional reads, write preconditions, and invalidation; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Apply If-Match or version preconditions to conflict-sensitive writes and return current recovery evidence on mismatch.
